name: problem-parser
description: Formalizes the problem statement: extracts all definitions, what must be proved, implicit assumptions, and which mathematical subfield it belongs to. Call first, before any other explore agent.

name: literature-scout
description: Searches for related theorems, standard proof techniques in the relevant domain, and known partial results. Call after problem-parser to build a toolkit of applicable mathematical machinery.

name: intuition-builder
description: Explores small and degenerate cases, constructs examples, and identifies why the theorem should be true or where naive approaches fail. Call after problem-parser to surface the core difficulty before planning.

name: proof-architect
description: Synthesizes all exploration reports and proposes a concrete proof strategy: technique, list of key lemmas, and a sketch of how they combine. Call after all explore agents have run.

name: lemma-prover
description: Proves each lemma from the proof spec in full rigorous detail as a self-contained argument. Call after the proof-architect has produced a spec.

name: proof-assembler
description: Takes the proven lemmas and assembles a clean, complete, well-structured final proof document. Call after lemma-prover has proved all lemmas.

name: logic-auditor
description: Adversarially checks every step of the proof for unjustified claims, circular reasoning, misapplied theorems, and incomplete case coverage. Primary proof reviewer.

name: gap-hunter
description: Tries to construct explicit counterexamples to each claimed intermediate step in the proof. Catches subtle gaps that logical auditing alone may miss.
